Perfect.

can't remember if it's 10 or 12, but like abstract images that we're using is the featured image for all of the blog posts.
So I will send you guys all of those.

So we'll just, we can do a test run.
I'll let you know when I've made those final updates to mine.
Oh, that's perfect.

Yeah, I'll definitely, I'll send you, like, email or a link, we'll walk through with, like, an update and a bit of explanation on why the categories are the way they are.
And again, I mentioned the categories.
Bye.

Shoot me an email.

I'm going to assign some of those to Diane, ones where I think, like, really it's just about an edit that, like, is the content, like, reading correctly and engaging?
It's not super technical.

And then any of the super technical ones where I think, oh, yeah, no, really, I need to do that, I'll pause it in the kind of, like, review queue or say, like, this will be assigned out to someone else as and when possible.
But that should give you a clear review of, yeah, expectations about that.
Perfect.
That was helpful to know.
And then in terms of, we've got these sort of two, what we're thinking of as the calibration articles in the queue and ready.
I think they're assigned to Chris, but whoever for.

Your team wants to take a look at these just to make sure with this new product, you know, competitive positioning document that we developed after that meeting to make sure it's kind of doing a good job and taking the right approach.
And then David wanted to kind of prioritize these straight up comparison articles, which I think will also be more straightforward based on kind of the information that you gave us.
So those are the ones that we're working on now and that we're kind of prioritizing first.

So we kind of kept going with content production this week, but it might make sense to kind of pause next week until you get a chance to take a look at these and offer feedback because I don't want us to get into where we have like this huge backlog queue that's taking the wrong angle and then, you know, you end up reviewing articles that aren't right.
So it feels like it's worth taking the right, to kind of dive into these and make sure they're taking the

Right approach, and we'll deliver these as well, but then we might wait for your feedback until we start running more, so you don't get a Christmas present of a bunch of articles that are kind of taking the wrong approach.
I can probably comment on those, Bailey.
I had a chance to review those two articles.
I thought they were great, by the way.
It's just like small corrections around terminology that I have to make.

But one thing I wanted to ask, especially about the pricing vendor comparison article, in terms of writing style, I noticed that we tend to, when talking about the competitors, tend to list a few of their strengths and capabilities, then we dive into how HX is better, where they're missing out, which made it read a bit like, obviously, it's not supposed to be completely objective.
But if this is going public, that was sort of a concern for me, that in a sense that we're too HX-sided.
So I wanted to ask, since also,
So our concern with this is like, we're doing this for SEO and GEO.
In that case, that might be completely fine.
So I wanted to like discuss this with you.
I think I would trust your point of view.
I every brand and company has a slightly different point of view on how kind of product forward they want these kind of comparison articles to be.
And that's why sometimes I think it takes some tweaks to make sure that the way we're approaching it fits with your brand.
Like, think you want it to feel like a useful piece of content for whoever's making this search.
I don't think it has to be totally neutral because, you know, it lives on your company's blog.
And like, you should have a point of view on these other companies and their products.
So I think that's okay for it to sort of have a point of view.
So for me, the baseline is like, what a person who's coming here searching this term and they land on this article, are they going to find it kind of full of?
Thank you.
Is it going to be useful to them?
I think that's kind of like the most important question to ask.
And I think being totally neutral is, I think, A, kind of impossible and would feel when it's on a branded website.
People wouldn't trust that anyway.

And I also think it's kind of not what you want because it's an opportunity for you to not sell, but to have your point of view on where these other tools are maybe falling short and therefore, like, why your company exists, why your product exists.
I agree, yes.

The only thing I would say is, like, for it to also function as, like, a thought leadership piece, as you said, I would say, like, it would be good for us to just do a small tweak on a few of the competitor sections just to give them a bit more credit on, like, some of the capabilities they have and then go into, like, our positioning against them.
And that totally makes sense.
And if you want to call those out and we can also, from just, like, the guidelines, kind of...

Make them increase, like, either the number of features or, like, making sure, like, the percentage in terms of how much time they spend talking about HX versus competitors, like, make that feel a little more neutral is something we can control on.

And apologies, if you knew this already, Mehmet, or if they looked at it, but, like, whenever you were making edits or, like, suggestions or feedback to these, do it as suggested edits to help for training for the AI engine to be able to see, like, what was the original, what's your suggested edit, like, any feedback.
Or commentary you have on, like, why that is the edit that you've made and, like, why it's important or meaningful.
All of that is, like, really helpful training data, so don't just, like, delete and redraft it because we lose all that insight that you could ever like.
Yeah, thank you.
Would you recommend just leaving comments on the page then instead of making edits directly?
I think it depends what it is.
Like, I think feedback is really helpful on, you know, bigger picture stuff.
Like, if you just want to highlight a second.
And say, you know, this section focuses too much on this tool's drawbacks and not enough on, like, the useful features.
Or, like, this section is too salesy.
I think that's a little bit easier for you and you're not kind of stuck worried about the exact wording.
Because I think, you know, when we're training these models, those are the things that we're really looking to update so that you're not the goal.
So you don't have to leave the same feedback on every single article, right?
So, but there may be some things where it's like, we should never use this word.
And then, yeah, you could delete it and tell us, like, not to use that word.
But I think anytime you're just leaving high-level feedback on approach, probably a comment is easier.
But whatever you want to do is fine.
But don't feel like you have to go in and make the line edits yourself, is what I would say.
Okay, good deal.
I already had my comments.
I wasn't sure what's the best way of, like, communicating them.
But I'll leave those as comments.
Yeah, that's perfect.
Yeah, also wanted to talk about the source material we sent over for competitors.
you.

Any concerns there?

Usually they're like asking, they're searching a specific term, they're looking for an answer to a specific question, and that's kind of the way they land on it.

So we're not talking in theory here.

Um.

No, ahead.

So if you can tag us, we'll take a look.
Okay, I can follow up with the list after this, because I'm not sure you're shared on Airtable either.
No.

Because I think it will take much longer if we reverse that.
And it's like, oh, yeah, we take a stab at it first, and then you guys stuff it up through the pipeline.

And I'll show you, and like it's.

And then next week, Jacob, one of our managing directors, will be on the call, and he'll be able to kind of talk through the opportunity, answer any questions you have.
And then...

Okay.
So I think that is it from my end.
know we kind of covered a lot.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on website relaunch, content reviews, and future strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=47.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Production Paused:** Production is on hold next week to allow for a full review of two "calibration" articles. This ensures the new competitive positioning is correct before scaling.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=287.0)
  - [**Comparison Article Strategy:** Articles will be HX-forward to match user search intent, but will be refined to give competitors more credit. This balances SEO goals with thought leadership.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
  - [**Website Relaunch Follow-up:** Blog auto-publishing will be tested after Franki finalizes new categories and featured images. Growth X will then update its Looker dashboard for aligned analytics.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=81.0)
  - [**PSEO Proposal:** A proposal for a high-volume (10 pages/week) programmatic SEO project was shared. A decision is needed to scope the required design work and plan the next content batch.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)

## Topics

### Content Review & Feedback Process

  - [**Franki's Holiday Coverage:** Franki is OOO (Dec 17–Jan 3). Diane will cover non-technical reviews; technical articles will be paused.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=242.0)
  - [**AI Training Feedback:** To train the AI model, use suggested edits for specific changes and comments for high-level feedback (e.g., "too salesy"). Do not delete and re-draft, as this loses valuable training data.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=554.0)

### Comparison Article Strategy

  - [**Issue:** Mehmet noted the first draft of the "pricing vendor comparison" article was too HX-sided.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
  - [**Decision:** The HX-forward style is acceptable, as it aligns with user search intent and provides a clear point of view. However, articles will be refined to give competitors more credit, improving the content's thought leadership quality.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=384.0)
  - [**Source Material:** Mehmet will send updated competitor positioning documents tomorrow. Growth X will use this to update its internal artifacts and re-run this week's articles, delaying delivery to early next week.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1441.0)

### Website Relaunch & Auto-Publishing

  - [**Blog Auto-Publishing:** A test is pending Franki's finalization of blog categories and featured images tomorrow.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=97.0)
      - [**Featured Images:** A set of 10–12 abstract images will be used for all posts.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=116.0)
      - [**Engineering Support:** Alec Kirkland (Growth X) is on standby to assist with the Framer connection if needed.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=211.0)
  - [**Analytics Alignment:** Growth X will update its Looker dashboard to align with the new blog categories, ensuring accurate performance tracking.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=148.0)

### Future Content Strategy

  - [**High-Level Topics:** David had concerns about the AI pipeline's ability to write complex, market-level articles (e.g., "AI insurance platforms for PNC").](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1092.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Growth X sees these as high-value keywords with weak current SERP results, representing a major opportunity.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1152.0)
      - [**Path Forward:** Growth X will run one topic through the pipeline as a test. Deyan and Franki will then review it to determine if the output is a viable starting point.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1183.0)
  - [**PSEO (Programmatic SEO) Proposal:**](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)
      - [**Details:** A proposal for a high-volume (10 pages/week) project was shared. The first batch is a 6-week project on "lines of business."](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)
      - [**Action:** A decision is needed to scope the required design work for the page template and plan the next content batch. Jacob (Growth X) will join next week's call to discuss.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1290.0)

### Admin & Logistics

  - [**AI Citation Tracking:** Growth X's tool tracks AI citations for the entire Hyperexponential brand, not just content. Bailey will export and share historical reports.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=890.0)
  - [**Content OS Access:** Mehmet requested access to Content OS; Bailey will add all team members.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1513.0)
  - [**Holiday Schedule:**](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1530.0)
      - [**Hyperexponential:** Franki OOO (Dec 17–Jan 3); Mehmet available.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1530.0)
      - [**Growth X:** Closed (Dec 25–Jan 1); an async update will be sent before the break.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1530.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Franki:**](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=242.0)

  - [**Growth X:**](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1472.0)

      - [Export and send historical AI citation reports.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=890.0)
      - [Grant Content OS access to the Hyperexponential team.](https://fathom.video/share/3H4imAz98S4gQ2xCw7TfyHc4xBHWZ9H8?tab=summary&timestamp=1513.0)
